notes: Label preceding a Hebrew/Greek lemma in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label preceding a short gloss in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label preceding the part of speech in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label preceding a Strong's number in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label preceding the morphology code in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label preceding the notes list in the Enhanced Resource word tooltip.
Label that identifies a footnote in the scripture pane note popup.
Label that identifies an endnote in the scripture pane note popup.
Label that identifies a cross-reference in the scripture pane note popup.
Placeholder shown when a note has no body text.
Heading above the list of verse references inside a note.
ARIA label for the scripture pane while it is loading.
Title shown when no chapter is loaded in the scripture pane.
Body shown when no chapter is loaded in the scripture pane.
Title shown when scripture pane fails to load.
Status text shown when a word filter is active in the scripture pane.
Placeholder text rendered in place of the real scripture editor during the design phase.
Header above the footnote list in the scripture pane.
{resourceName} is replaced with the resource short name.
ARIA label for the close button on a dismissible warning ribbon.
ARIA label / tooltip for the per-row expand toggle in resource lists.
ARIA label / tooltip for the per-row collapse toggle in resource lists.
ARIA label for a resource list while it is loading.
Inline label preceding a sense definition.
Inline label preceding the gloss list of a sense.
Inline label preceding the part of speech of a sense.
ARIA label for a sense flagged as non-relevant for the current verse.
ARIA label for the Expand all entries action.
ARIA label for the Collapse all entries action.
Empty state when there is no data for the current scope. {scope} is the localized scope label.
Empty state when the filter word matches no entries in scope. {word} and {scope} are replaced.
Empty state when the filter word does not occur in the current scope. {word} and {scope} are replaced.
Heading shown when the active dictionary is SDBH (Hebrew).
Heading shown when the active dictionary is SDBG (Greek).
Tooltip / ARIA label for the per-entry occurrence count badge.
ARIA label for clicking a source text link to filter the form.
Context menu item: Copy the surface form (Hebrew/Greek + transliteration).
Context menu item: Copy the lemma (Hebrew/Greek + transliteration).
Switch label: hide senses flagged as non-relevant in the current verse.
Group label inside related lexemes: lexemes related by shared gloss.
Group label inside related lexemes: lexemes related by shared semantic domain.
Group label inside related lexemes: lexemes related by lexical relation.
Placeholder when an expanded entry has no detail data to display.
Label for the 'Back to list' button at the top of the dictionary entry detail drawer.
Heading and ARIA label for the Encyclopedia research tab.
ARIA label for the Expand all entries action in EncyclopediaTab.
ARIA label for the Collapse all entries action in EncyclopediaTab.
Empty state when no encyclopedia entries match the current scope. {scope} is the localized scope label.
Empty state when the filter word matches no encyclopedia entries in scope. {word} and {scope} are replaced.
Empty state when the filter word does not occur in the current scope. {word} and {scope} are replaced.
Encyclopedia context menu item: Copy the surface form.
Encyclopedia context menu item: Copy the lemma.
ARIA label for clicking a source-text link in the encyclopedia entry row.
ARIA label for clicking an article title link in the encyclopedia entry row.
ARIA label / alt text prefix for inline thumbnail images on an encyclopedia row.
Footer link in expanded encyclopedia detail that opens the full ArticleViewer drawer.
ARIA label for the article loading skeleton in the expanded encyclopedia detail.
Placeholder when an expanded encyclopedia entry has no article data to display.
Reserved label for V1/V2 format indicator (currently unused; backend abstracts the difference).
Section heading above the cross-references list inside an encyclopedia article.
Section heading above the verse-reference list inside an encyclopedia article.
Alt-text prefix for inline images inside an encyclopedia article.
ARIA label for clicking an inline article image to open the MediaViewer.
ARIA label for clicking a See also cross-reference link in an article.
ARIA label for clicking a verse reference link inside an article.
Placeholder shown when ArticleRenderer receives no article data.
Inline notice shown at the end of a preview-mode article when paragraphs were omitted.
ARIA label / alt text for a media thumbnail. {title} is replaced with the entry title.
ARIA label for a media thumbnail Skeleton placeholder shown while the bytes are still being fetched (BHV-359 deferred loading).
Heading and ARIA label for the Media Images research tab.
Empty state message for the Media Images tab when no images match the current scope. {scope} is the localized scope label.
Singular media-files count label shown in the Media Images tab header. {count} is replaced with the integer count (always 1 here).
Plural media-files count label shown in the Media Images tab header. {count} is the integer count.
Heading and ARIA label for the Media Maps research tab (Satellite Bible Atlas).
Empty state message for the Media Maps tab when no Satellite Bible Atlas entries match the current scope. {scope} is the localized scope label.
Singular map-files count label shown in the Media Maps tab header. {count} is replaced with the integer count (always 1 here).
Plural map-files count label shown in the Media Maps tab header. {count} is the integer count.
Drawer title for the in-tab MediaViewer (image overlay).
ARIA label for the MediaViewer close button (X icon).
ARIA label for the MediaViewer previous-image button. Hidden when there is only one image.
ARIA label for the MediaViewer next-image button. Hidden when there is only one image.
ARIA label for the MediaViewer zoom-in button (+ icon).
ARIA label for the MediaViewer zoom-out button (- icon). Disabled at 1x zoom.
Status text shown over the MediaViewer canvas while the image bytes are loading.
Alt text template for the MediaViewer image. {title} is replaced with the image title.
ARIA-live counter announcing the displayed image position. {current} and {total} are integers.
ARIA-live announcement of the current zoom level. {percent} is the zoom percentage (100 = 1x).
DrawerTitle text for the ArticleViewer overlay (also the screen-reader fallback when no article is loaded).
ARIA label for the ArticleViewer back button (arrow-left icon, closes drawer).
ARIA label for the ArticleViewer close button (X icon).
ARIA label for the ArticleViewer previous-article button. Hidden when no sequence is available.
ARIA label for the ArticleViewer next-article button. Hidden when no sequence is available.
Status text shown while the ArticleViewer is fetching article content.
Placeholder shown when the ArticleViewer is open but no article data is available.
Section heading above the list of abbreviations (with hover tooltips) inside the ArticleViewer.
ARIA label for the ArticleViewer abbreviation hover surface. Activating reveals the full text via tooltip.
DrawerTitle text for the SemanticDomainViewer overlay.
Screen-reader description for the SemanticDomainViewer drawer. Announces the drawer's purpose.
ARIA label for the SemanticDomainViewer back button (arrow-left icon, closes drawer).
ARIA label for the SemanticDomainViewer close button (X icon).
ARIA label announced for the semantic-domain hierarchy tree (role=tree).
ARIA label for the breadcrumb navigation landmark.
Root-level label rendered as the first breadcrumb item (e.g. 'All').
Visible separator string between breadcrumb items (e.g. '>'). Decorative; aria-hidden.
ARIA label / tooltip for the per-node expand toggle in the semantic domain tree.
ARIA label / tooltip for the per-node collapse toggle in the semantic domain tree.
ARIA label for a domain entry whose hasArticle is true. Activating closes the drawer and opens the ArticleViewer.
ARIA label for a domain category whose hasArticle is false. Activating focuses the tree on that category.
Section heading above the dictionary entries filtered by the currently focused semantic domain. {domainLabel} is the current domain label.
ARIA label for the filtered-entries ResourceList inside SemanticDomainViewer.
Empty-state message when the focused domain has no associated dictionary entries.
ARIA label for the filtered entries list while loading.
Status text shown while the SemanticDomainViewer is fetching the domain tree.
Placeholder shown when the SemanticDomainViewer is open but no domain tree data is available.
Inline label preceding the gloss list of a dictionary entry inside the SemanticDomainViewer.
Title of the 'Getting started in Enhanced Resources' guide dialog. Mirrors PT9 string MarbleGuideForm_1.
Screen-reader description of the MarbleGuide dialog. Not visible on screen; provides context for the Radix Dialog primitive.
Opening paragraph of the guide explaining what an Enhanced Resource is. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_2.
Paragraph instructing the user how to use the colored buttons at the top of the resource. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_3.
Paragraph describing research-term highlighting. Contains the {colorWord} placeholder which is replaced with a color chip showing the localized 'blue' word. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_4.
The localized name of the highlight color used for research terms (English: 'blue'). Displayed inside the color chip in the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_5.
Paragraph describing how to research Biblical Terms in the user's project. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_6.
Paragraph describing found-rendering highlighting. Contains the {colorWord} placeholder. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_7.
The localized name of the highlight color used for found renderings (English: 'gray'). PT9 MarbleGuideForm_8.
Paragraph describing needs-rendering highlighting. Contains {colorWord} and {helpLink} placeholders. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_9.
The localized name of the highlight color used for needs-rendering attention (English: 'orange'). PT9 MarbleGuideForm_10.
Anchor text for the help-topic link inside the orange-highlight paragraph. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_11.
Heading above the research tab icon grid. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_12.
Caption below the dictionary tab icon in the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_13.
Caption below the encyclopedia tab icon in the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_14.
Caption below the media tab icon in the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_15.
Caption below the maps tab icon in the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_16.
Reminder paragraph telling users to keep their resources up to date. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_17.
Paragraph explaining how to reopen the guide via the info icon. Contains the {infoIcon} placeholder. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_18.
Anchor text for the 'More help...' link at the bottom of the guide. PT9 MarbleGuideForm_19.
Label for the Close button in the MarbleGuide dialog footer.
Label for the 'Don't show this again' checkbox in the MarbleGuide dialog footer.

en.%enhancedResources_scripturePane_emptyDe: Select a verse reference to begin.
en.%enhancedResources_scripturePane_editorP: Scripture editor placeholder. The integrated read-only editor will replace this area in a later iteration.
en.%enhancedResources_ribbon_reviewStatus_d: The text is complete, but research materials and links may contain errors.
en.%enhancedResources_ribbon_metadata_descr: This Enhanced Resource needs a metadata update before it can be used. Click
en.%enhancedResources_ribbon_updateAvailabl: A newer version of this Enhanced Resource is available.
en.%enhancedResources_copyrightOverlay_empt: No copyright information is available for this resource.
en.%enhancedResources_shell_emptyDescriptio: Open an Enhanced Resource from the Paratext menu to begin.
en.%enhancedResources_dictionary_occurrence: Occurrences in current scope (click to navigate)
en.%enhancedResources_article_truncatedNoti: (Preview - additional paragraphs available in full view.)
en.%enhancedResources_semanticDomain_descri: Browse the semantic-domain hierarchy and the dictionary entries that belong to the focused domain.
en.%enhancedResources_semanticDomain_filter: Dictionary entries for the current domain
No dictionary entries are associated with this domain.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_descripti: Tutorial explaining word highlighting colors, research tab icons, and basic navigation in Enhanced Resources.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_overview%: An enhanced resource is a resource text in which many words and phrases have been linked, word-by-word, to the original Hebrew and Greek texts. These words and phrases have research material linked to them.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_chooseMat: Click one or more colored buttons at the top of the enhanced resource to select which words to highlight in the text. Point to or click highlighted words to get more information about them.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_aboutBlue: Research Terms: A word highlighted in {colorWord} indicates that it can be researched. It may or may not also be in the Biblical terms list associated with your project.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_aboutBibl: Biblical Terms: To research Biblical Terms in your project, first open your project and then choose it from the drop-down at the top of the Scripture pane. This enables the Found and Problem buttons. Open a Biblical Terms Renderings window to track and edit renderings.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_aboutGray: A word highlighted in {colorWord} indicates that a valid rendering for this term has been found in this verse, in your project.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_aboutOran: A word highlighted in {colorWord} needs your attention because an approved rendering for this term is "missing" in this verse (though Paratext may have found a guessed rendering). For details, see {helpLink}.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_tabsHeade: Tabs: Tabs with black icons in the Research pane contain information about words in the current scope:
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_updateFre: Enhanced resources may be updated frequently. Be sure to keep your resources up to date.
en.%enhancedResources_marbleGuide_howToOpen: You can open this guide at any time using the {infoIcon} icon at the top right.
